description: RFC Test Matrix Coverage — add missing tests to reach ≥90% MUST coverage across RFC 7230, RFC 7541, and RFC 7540

userStories.title: RFC 7230 §3.2 — Header field edge cases for Http11Decoder
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Http11DecoderTests to cover the header field rules from RFC 7230 §3.2 so that I can trust the decoder handles real-world edge cases.
acceptanceCriteria: // RFC 7230 §3.2 — OWS trimming: Decode_Header_OWS_Trimmed — header 'X-Foo:   bar   ' decodes value as 'bar'
// RFC 7230 §3.2 — empty value: Decode_Header_EmptyValue_Accepted — 'X-Empty:\r\n' → header present with empty string value
// RFC 7230 §3.2 — case-insensitive name: Decode_Header_CaseInsensitiveName — 'HOST: example.com' accessible as 'Host'
// RFC 7230 §3.2 — multi-value: Decode_Header_MultipleValuesForSameName_Preserved — two Accept: lines → both values available
// RFC 9112 §5.1 — obs-fold: Decode_Header_ObsFold_Http11_IsError — line starting with SP/HT → parse error (HttpDecoderException)
dotnet test passes

userStories.title: RFC 7230 §3.3 — Message body edge cases for Http11Decoder
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Http11DecoderTests to cover conflicting body indicators and invalid Content-Length values.
acceptanceCriteria: // RFC 7230 §3.3 — Decode_ConflictingHeaders_ChunkedTakesPrecedence — Transfer-Encoding: chunked + Content-Length: 5 → body parsed via chunked
// RFC 7230 §3.3 — Decode_MultipleContentLength_DifferentValues_Throws — two Content-Length with different values → throws HttpDecoderException
// RFC 7230 §3.3 — Decode_NegativeContentLength_HandledGracefully — Content-Length: -1 → no exception (empty body or error)
// RFC 7230 §3.3 — Decode_NoBodyIndicator_EmptyBody — no Content-Length or Transfer-Encoding on 200 → empty body, success
dotnet test passes

userStories.title: RFC 7230 §4.1 — Chunked encoding error cases for Http11Decoder
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Http11DecoderTests to cover error paths in chunked transfer encoding.
acceptanceCriteria: // RFC 7230 §4.1 — Decode_InvalidChunkSize_ReturnsError — chunk size line 'xyz\r\n' → HttpDecoderException or returns false
// RFC 7230 §4.1 — Decode_ChunkSizeTooLarge_ReturnsError — chunk size '999999999999\r\n' → HttpDecoderException (overflow)
// RFC 7230 §4.1 — Decode_ChunkedWithTrailer_TrailerHeadersPresent — trailer 'X-Trailer: value' → accessible via response.TrailingHeaders
dotnet test passes

userStories.title: RFC 7231 §6.1 — Status code edge cases for Http11Decoder
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Http11DecoderTests to cover boundary status codes and the empty reason phrase case.
acceptanceCriteria: // RFC 7231 §6.1 — Decode_Status599_ParsedAsCustom — 'HTTP/1.1 599 Custom\r\n\r\n' → status code 599 decoded successfully
// RFC 7231 §6.1 — Decode_Status600_ReturnsError — 'HTTP/1.1 600 Invalid\r\n\r\n' → TryParseStatusLine returns false (≥600 rejected)
// RFC 7231 §6.1 — Decode_EmptyReasonPhrase_Accepted — 'HTTP/1.1 200 \r\n\r\n' → status 200 decoded, reason phrase empty or whitespace
dotnet test passes

userStories.title: RFC 7541 §2.3 — Dynamic table eviction
userStories.description: As a developer, I want explicit tests for HPACK dynamic table eviction so that I can trust table overflow is handled correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: // RFC 7541 §2.3 — DynamicTable_Eviction_OldestEntryRemovedWhenFull — fill table, add one more entry → oldest no longer accessible
// RFC 7541 §2.3 — DynamicTable_EvictionOrder_NewestSurvives — fill A, B, C; evict until space for D → D and most recent entries survive
// RFC 7541 §2.3 — DynamicTable_SizeTooBig_ThrowsHpackException — negative SetMaxAllowedTableSize → HpackException thrown
dotnet test passes

userStories.title: RFC 7541 §5.1 — Integer representation edge cases
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests for HPACK integer encoding boundary values so that overflow and truncation are validated.
acceptanceCriteria: // RFC 7541 §5.1 — ReadInteger_FitsInPrefix_SingleByte — value 5, prefix 5 bits → reads 1 byte, returns 5
// RFC 7541 §5.1 — ReadInteger_MultiByteEncoding_DecodedCorrectly — encode 1337 (RFC example) → decoded as 1337
// RFC 7541 §5.1 — ReadInteger_MaxValue_Accepted — value (1<<28)-1 encoded and decoded without error
// RFC 7541 §5.1 — ReadInteger_Overflow_ThrowsHpackException — value (1<<30) → HpackException with overflow message
// RFC 7541 §5.1 — ReadInteger_TruncatedData_ThrowsHpackException — multi-byte integer with no stop bit → HpackException
dotnet test passes

userStories.title: RFC 7541 §5.2 — String representation edge cases
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests for empty strings, large strings, and invalid Huffman data in HPACK string encoding.
acceptanceCriteria: // RFC 7541 §5.2 — Decode_EmptyLiteralString_ReturnsEmptyString — H=0, length=0 → header with empty value decoded
// RFC 7541 §5.2 — Decode_EmptyHuffmanString_ReturnsEmptyString — H=1, length=0 → header with empty value decoded
// RFC 7541 §5.2 — Decode_LargeString_8KB_DecodedCorrectly — literal string 8192 chars → decoded without error
// RFC 7541 §5.2 — Decode_InvalidHuffman_ThrowsHpackException — H=1 with malformed Huffman bytes → HpackException
dotnet test passes

userStories.title: RFC 7541 §6.x — Literal header indexing modes
userStories.description: As a developer, I want explicit tests for the three literal header indexing modes (incremental, without indexing, never indexed) in isolation.
acceptanceCriteria: // RFC 7541 §6.2 — Decode_LiteralWithoutIndexing_NotAddedToDynamicTable — without-indexing pattern (0000xxxx) → header decoded but NOT added to dynamic table
// RFC 7541 §6.2.3 — Decode_NeverIndexed_DecodedWithNeverIndexFlag — never-indexed pattern (0001xxxx) → decoded HpackHeader.NeverIndex == true
// RFC 7541 §6.1 — Decode_IndexOutOfRange_ThrowsHpackException — index > static+dynamic count → HpackException
// RFC 7541 §6.2.3 — Encode_SensitiveHeader_Authorization_UsesNeverIndex — 'authorization' header → encoded byte uses never-index prefix (0001xxxx)
// RFC 7541 §6.2.3 — Encode_SensitiveHeader_Cookie_UsesNeverIndex — same for 'cookie'
dotnet test passes

userStories.title: RFC 7541 Appendix C — Full example sequences C.2–C.6
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all RFC 7541 Appendix C example sequences tested with exact byte verification so that I have a ground truth for HPACK correctness.
acceptanceCriteria: // RFC 7541 C.2 — Decode_AppendixC2_AllThreeRequests — three sequential requests decoded in order; dynamic table state carried across requests; each decoded header list matches RFC exactly
// RFC 7541 C.3 — Decode_AppendixC3_AllThreeRequests_WithHuffman — same as C.2 but Huffman-encoded; HuffmanCodec must produce exact bytes from RFC
// RFC 7541 C.4 — Decode_AppendixC4_AllThreeResponses — three sequential responses without Huffman; eviction occurs in C.4.3 → verify entry count
// RFC 7541 C.5 — Decode_AppendixC5_AllThreeResponses_WithHuffman — C.5 with Huffman
// RFC 7541 C.6 — Decode_AppendixC6_AllThreeResponses_WithHuffmanAndEviction — C.6 with Huffman and eviction; verify evicted entries match RFC expected table state
Tests use a single HpackDecoder instance across all three calls per sequence (shared dynamic table)
dotnet test passes
userStories.notes: Place in a new HpackAppendixCTests.cs file for clarity.

userStories.title: RFC 7540 §4.1 — Frame format validation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Http2DecoderTests to cover unknown frame types and the frame size limit so that the decoder handles RFC 7540 §4.1 correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: // RFC 7540 §4.1 — Decode_UnknownFrameType_IsIgnored — frame with type byte 0x0A (unknown) → TryDecode returns false/empty, no exception, decoder remains usable
// RFC 7540 §4.1 — Decode_FrameExceedingMaxFrameSize_ThrowsFrameSizeError — payload > 16384 → Http2Exception with Http2ErrorCode.FrameSizeError
dotnet test passes

userStories.title: RFC 7540 §6.1 — DATA frame edge cases
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Http2DecoderTests to cover empty DATA frames and DATA with padding.
acceptanceCriteria: // RFC 7540 §6.1 — Decode_EmptyDataFrame_Accepted — DATA frame with payload length=0 on open stream → accepted, body is empty byte array
// RFC 7540 §6.1 — Decode_DataFrame_WithPadding_PaddingStripped — DATA with PADDED flag, 5 bytes padding → only actual data bytes returned
Construct padded DATA frames as raw bytes (use the existing DataFrame pattern as reference)
dotnet test passes

userStories.title: RFC 7540 §6.2 — HEADERS frame edge cases
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Http2DecoderTests to cover HEADERS with PRIORITY flag and padding.
acceptanceCriteria: // RFC 7540 §6.2 — Decode_HeadersFrame_WithPriorityFlag_ParsedCorrectly — HEADERS with PRIORITY flag (5 extra bytes) → headers decoded correctly, priority bytes skipped
// RFC 7540 §6.2 — Decode_HeadersFrame_WithPadding_PaddingStripped — HEADERS with PADDED flag → header block decoded without padding
Construct padded/priority HEADERS frames as raw bytes directly in test setup
dotnet test passes

userStories.title: RFC 7540 §6.9 — Multiple CONTINUATION frames
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Http2DecoderTests to verify that more than two CONTINUATION frames can be reassembled correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: // RFC 7540 §6.10 — Decode_ThreeContinuationFrames_AllReassembled — one HEADERS + two CONTINUATION frames → response decoded correctly with all headers present
dotnet test passes

userStories.title: RFC 7540 §3.5 — Connection preface magic bytes
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a test that verifies the HTTP/2 connection preface bytes match the RFC exactly.
acceptanceCriteria: // RFC 7540 §3.5 — BuildConnectionPreface_MagicBytes_ExactlyMatchRfc7540 — byte-for-byte comparison: 50 52 49 20 2A 20 48 54 54 50 2F 32 2E 30 0D 0A 0D 0A 53 4D 0D 0A 0D 0A (24 bytes)
dotnet test passes
